title: Durable Long-Running Work and Operations · Recovery Bay
summary: Operate a multi-hour or multi-day agent task through interruptions. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a durable runbook and restartable workflow and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate durable orchestration state from ephemeral model turns.
Use scheduled checks or heartbeats only when timing or external state requires them.
Wait or subscribe for expected external changes without classifying unchanged state as failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Operator Handoff evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Operator Handoff assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Expose current phase, next wake condition, authority, costs, and safe interventions. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Preserve authority, safety, accessibility, traceability, and stakeholder consequences alongside technical correctness.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: loops long running. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Operator Handoff, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a durable runbook and restartable workflow.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Operators only need a binary running indicator. This fails the Operator Handoff contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
